_comment: Give ground (#983): an explicit player-facing withdrawal order. Two equal-reach Infantry regiments (both sword reach 26wu) so #981's passive reach-asymmetric standoff never applies to either side (it's unconditionally zero at equal reach) -- any retreat shown here is purely the give-ground order's own effect, not the passive standoff. The player's Infantry (team 0) starts stationary; the enemy Infantry (team 1) auto-advances and locks in melee. Once engaged, the player selects the unit and issues Ctrl+backtick (Give ground) -- the unit then backs away from its nearest enemy at a constant rate while continuing to fight (soldier counts on both sides keep declining). Driven live through the real SelectionManager by tools/demo/DemoInputRecorder.gd. World coordinates; physics ticks (60/s).
